content.claim: Selective gimnázium tracking schools raise short-term test scores of admitted students but appear to depress achievement outcomes for students left in standard schools, and long-run labor-market gains from the short-term selection advantage are not clearly established.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Based on a single empirical research strand (Horn 2013 and CEU thesis follow-up), not multiply replicated internationally
The zero-sum relocation finding is inferred from value-added comparison rather than direct measurement of opportunity cost
Long-run disappearance of gains documented in thesis but not yet published in mainstream peer-reviewed outlets
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
